title: Seismic Event Detection System
subtitle: CNN + Transformer pipeline for earthquake detection & risk alerts
keyFeatures: Deep learning system combining CNN and Transformer architectures for earthquake detection, magnitude estimation and epicenter location prediction
Power Spectral Density (PSD) features integrated for enhanced frequency-domain analysis
Multi-stage pipeline over STEAD seismic waveform data for event classification, geolocation and risk-based alerting
Interactive dashboard for seismic monitoring and prediction analysis
results: Improved magnitude prediction accuracy through PSD-based frequency features
End-to-end pipeline covering detection, classification, geolocation and alerting
challenges: Extracting reliable frequency-domain signal from noisy real-world waveform data
Combining CNN and Transformer representations without losing localization precision
lessonsLearned: Frequency-domain features (PSD) added real signal beyond raw waveform inputs
Visualization and evaluation tooling were as important as the model itself for a monitoring use case

title: InsightPDF — RAG Document Intelligence Platform
subtitle: Conversational Q&A and summarization over large PDFs
keyFeatures: Streamlit-based RAG app that ingests large PDFs and returns structured summaries plus conversational Q&A over document content
Modular LangChain backend for document classification, summarization, retrieval and conversational agents
ChromaDB + HuggingFace embeddings for persistent vector storage
Locally-orchestrated Ollama (Llama-3) LLM for grounded, source-based Q&A
results: Fully local RAG stack — no external LLM API dependency
Structured, section-aware summaries instead of flat text dumps
challenges: Keeping answers grounded to the source PDF and avoiding hallucination
Chunking long documents while preserving enough context for retrieval
lessonsLearned: Modular retrieval/summarization/classification stages made the backend easy to extend
Local LLM orchestration (Ollama) is a viable, cost-free alternative to hosted APIs for RAG prototypes

title: Traffic Signal Management
subtitle: Multi-agent reinforcement learning for adaptive traffic control
keyFeatures: PPO-based RL model that optimizes traffic signal timings using SUMO-simulated real-world traffic data
Custom reward strategies that prioritize high-traffic lanes to reduce overall urban congestion
results: Adaptive signal timing that responds to simulated real-time traffic load
Reward shaping tuned specifically toward congestion reduction on high-traffic lanes
challenges: Designing a reward function that balances fairness across lanes with overall throughput
Getting stable multi-agent training convergence in a shared traffic environment
lessonsLearned: Reward shaping mattered more than raw model capacity for this environment
SUMO simulation fidelity was key to getting policies that would generalize

title: ArcFlow — Interactive Story Platform
subtitle: AI-driven branching narrative platform
keyFeatures: AI-driven story platform with dynamic branching choices
Genre/mood-aware generative API integration for narrative progression
results: Fully dynamic branching narratives generated on the fly rather than pre-scripted
Persistent, isolated story history per user
challenges: Keeping generated narrative continuity coherent across branching choices
Designing a data model that supports per-user branching story state
lessonsLearned: Mood/genre conditioning noticeably improved narrative consistency
Clean separation between story-state storage and generation logic simplified branching
